question: Did the model select Option B or explicitly identify the diagnosis as 'Hypersensitivity pneumonitis'?
Did the model correctly identify the image as a histological specimen of lung tissue (e.g., noting alveolar architecture)?
Did the model correctly describe the presence of interstitial inflammation, lymphocytic infiltration, or thickened alveolar septa?
Did the model correctly identify the presence of poorly formed granulomas or multinucleated giant cells within the tissue?
Did the model avoid hallucinating the presence of 'hyaline membranes' (a key feature of Diffuse Alveolar Damage)?
Did the model avoid hallucinating the presence of 'caseous necrosis' (a key feature of Tuberculosis)?
Did the model avoid hallucinating viral cytopathic effects (e.g., inclusions) or unprovided diagnostic results (e.g., AFB staining, radiology findings)?
Did the model correctly interpret the clinical history (symptoms occurring at work and abating at home) as indicative of an occupational or environmental exposure?
Did the model synthesize the clinical history (recurrent/occupational symptoms) and the histological findings (granulomatous inflammation) to justify the diagnosis of Hypersensitivity Pneumonitis?

Did the model provide logical reasoning to rule out alternatives (such as Miliary Tuberculosis or Diffuse Alveolar Damage) based on the absence of their specific histological hallmarks (caseation or hyaline membranes)?
